recommendation: SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework) is increasingly required for defense PM roles. Your Agile and large-team experience is directly relevant. SAFe Agilist is the leadership-level cert.
veteran_discount_notes: Some SAFe training providers offer veteran pricing

recommendation: Free Jira training at Atlassian University. Similar concept to military task tracking systems. Free cloud version available for small teams to practice.
Kanban uses visual boards to track work flow. If you used any task tracking board or status board, you have used Kanban concepts.
Free Splunk training and certification available. Free Splunk instance for practice. Your log analysis or security monitoring experience is directly relevant.
veteran_discount_notes: Splunk offers military/veteran training programs

recommendation: CAPM is the entry-level PM cert if you do not yet qualify for PMP. Only requires 23 hours of PM education.
veteran_discount_notes: PMI offers military discount

recommendation: Certified Safety Professional requires 4-year degree + experience. Your military safety management experience counts toward the experience requirement.
Free and open source network analyzer. If you did any network troubleshooting in the military, Wireshark is the civilian standard.
Microsoft collaboration platform. Free training on Microsoft Learn. If you used any military knowledge management portal, SharePoint is similar.
HBSS (Host Based Security System) is the military endpoint security platform. Civilian equivalent is McAfee/Trellix ePO. Your HBSS experience translates directly.
Database query language used everywhere. Free courses available. If you managed any database or tracking system, SQL is the foundation.
Certified ScrumMaster is a 2-day course. Your military experience with iterative planning and team leadership maps directly to Scrum.
ITIL 4 Foundation covers IT service management. Your military operations and maintenance management experience maps directly to ITIL concepts.
Your Security+ and CySA+ combined with years of experience cover CASP+ domains. Consider pursuing if targeting senior security architect roles.
veteran_discount_notes: CompTIA offers military spouse and veteran pricing

recommendation: Enterprise IT service management platform. Free training and simulator available. Your military ticketing/maintenance tracking experience is relevant.
veteran_discount_notes: ServiceNow has a veteran hiring program and free training

recommendation: If you coordinated resources between departments, managed vendor relationships, or resolved conflicts, you have negotiation experience. Military resource competition requires constant negotiation.
If you briefed leadership, conducted training, or led meetings, you have public speaking experience. Military briefing experience is often more rigorous than civilian presentation skills.

If you worked in military cybersecurity (DIACAP, ACAS, STIGs, eMASS), you have worked with RMF concepts. Military uses RMF. Just make sure to use the term "NIST Risk Management Framework" on your resume.
Start with AWS Cloud Practitioner (foundational) or Solutions Architect Associate. Your infrastructure experience translates well to cloud.
veteran_discount_notes: AWS offers free re/Start program for military and veterans

recommendation: Your Security+, CySA+, or CASP+ demonstrate similar cybersecurity competency. Highlight these as equivalent experience in your cover letter. CISSP requires 5 years experience — your military service counts.
veteran_discount_notes: ISC2 offers military/veteran discounts and workforce programs

recommendation: Enterprise resource planning system. Free learning hub available. Your military logistics/supply chain experience maps to SAP modules.
If you used 3M/OMMS-NG (Navy), GCSS-Army, or NALCOMIS (aviation), you used a CMMS. These are equivalent to civilian systems like Maximo, SAP PM, or Fiix. Say "CMMS" on your resume.
If you participated in military emergency response, you operated under Incident Command System principles. ICS-100, 200, 700, and 800 are free online and validate what you already know.
If you briefed flag/general officers or senior executives, you have executive presence. Military bearing and communication discipline translate directly to boardroom presence.
Data visualization platform. Free Tableau Public version and extensive free training. Your briefing and data presentation experience is relevant.
Free and open source OS. Foundation for cybersecurity, cloud, and DevOps roles. If you used any military Unix systems, Linux is similar.
CCNP builds on CCNA. Your enterprise network experience from military service is a strong foundation. Requires CCNA or equivalent experience.
veteran_discount_notes: Cisco NetAcad offers military programs

recommendation: If you tracked readiness metrics, maintenance data, personnel statistics, or budget reports, you performed data analysis. Add this skill and learn Excel/Power BI to formalize it.
If you used military supply/logistics systems (Navy ERP, GCSS-Army, DLA systems), you used ERP platforms. Say "ERP" on your resume — it is the same concept.
If you conducted AARs, mishap investigations, or equipment failure analysis, you performed root cause analysis. Add this to your skills — it is highly valued.
HR and finance cloud platform. If you managed personnel or payroll systems in the military, concepts are similar.

Your military experience with iterative mission planning, AARs, and adapting to changing conditions IS Agile in practice. Add "Agile Methodology" to your skills. Consider CSM cert for formal validation.
Spreadsheet-based project management. If you are comfortable with Excel, Smartsheet is an easy transition.
If you handled HAZMAT in the military (DC, CBRN, environmental), you likely have equivalent training. May need 8-hour refresher for civilian certification.
If you led organizational transitions, implemented new processes, or managed personnel through deployments/restructures, you practiced change management. Add it.
OSHA 30-hour course is available online. Your military safety experience exceeds most civilian safety training.

Many employers accept equivalent experience in lieu of a degree. Your {{years}} years of military experience + certifications often equals or exceeds a bachelor's. Some employers accept CLEP credits. Check if the JD says "or equivalent experience."
veteran_discount_notes: CLEP exams are free for active duty military through DANTES

recommendation: Entry-level IT cert. If you have any hands-on IT experience from the military, A+ is achievable in 4-6 weeks of study.
veteran_discount_notes: CompTIA offers military/veteran pricing at 50% discount

recommendation: Free tutorials on Microsoft Learn. If you managed schedules or Gantt charts in the military, MS Project will feel familiar.
Free version available. Intuitive project management platform. Very learnable in a few hours.
GIAC certs (GSEC, GCIH, GCIA, etc.) are gold standard in cybersecurity. Your military cyber experience is relevant foundation. SANS courses are expensive but top quality.
veteran_discount_notes: SANS offers military discount and VET TEC programs through SANS.edu

recommendation: If you worked with any military communication or network systems, you already know most of this material. Strong foundation cert.
veteran_discount_notes: CompTIA offers military/veteran pricing at 50% discount

recommendation: Advanced Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, macros). Free training on Microsoft Learn. Your military reporting experience is a strong foundation.
Container orchestration platform. Free training at kubernetes.io. Growing requirement for cloud and DevOps roles.
Vulnerability scanner. Free Nessus Essentials version available. Your STIG/vulnerability scanning experience is equivalent.
If you participated in equipment procurement, program reviews, or milestone decisions, you have DoD acquisition experience. Familiarize yourself with the 5000 series if targeting acquisition roles.
veteran_discount_notes: DAU courses are free for military and government civilians

recommendation: Atlassian wiki/documentation tool. If you wrote SOPs, instructions, or maintained any documentation system, Confluence is similar.
Container platform. Free to use. Free training available. Foundational for modern DevOps and cloud roles.
Basic Life Support is a 1-day course. Most military medical personnel already have this or equivalent training.
Version control system used by every software team. Free. Essential for any technical role.
DevOps combines development and operations. Your military ops tempo and systems management experience is a foundation. Focus on learning the specific tools (Docker, Jenkins, Git).
Microsoft data visualization tool. Free desktop version. Integrates with Excel, which you likely already know.
Traditional sequential project management. Military planning processes (MDMP, naval planning process) follow waterfall methodology. Add this to your skills.
Open source SIEM alternative. Free training available. If you have Splunk experience, ELK concepts are similar.
CRM platform. Free Trailhead training is excellent. Salesforce Admin cert is achievable in 2-3 months and highly marketable.
veteran_discount_notes: Salesforce has dedicated veteran hiring programs (VetForce)

recommendation: Enterprise database and ERP platform. Free Oracle Academy training available. Your data management experience is relevant.
If you briefed senior leadership, coordinated with external agencies, or managed competing priorities from multiple departments, you managed stakeholders. Add this skill.
CI/CD automation server. Free and open source. Your process automation experience is a foundation.
Your PMP certification covers EVM fundamentals. If you tracked project budgets, schedules, and performance metrics, you used EVM concepts. Add "Earned Value Management" to your skills.
Free version available. Similar to Jira but simpler. If you know any task management tool, Asana takes 1-2 hours to learn.
Start with AZ-900 (fundamentals) then AZ-104 (administrator). Free training on Microsoft Learn. Your military IT experience maps directly.
veteran_discount_notes: Microsoft offers free certification exams for eligible veterans through MSFT Software & Systems Academy

recommendation: Most in-demand programming language. Free courses everywhere. Useful for automation, data analysis, and cybersecurity scripting.
Your experience serving fellow service members, working with external agencies, and supporting mission requirements IS customer service in a different context. Frame it that way.
ACAS is the military/DoD vulnerability scanning platform. Civilian equivalent is Nessus/Tenable.sc. Your ACAS experience directly translates — just say "Tenable/Nessus" on your resume.
Your military process improvement experience maps directly to Lean Six Sigma. Yellow Belt is free online, Green Belt is a strong resume addition.
Web application security testing tool. Free Community Edition available. Your pen testing experience is relevant foundation.

If you have a bachelor's, GI Bill covers master's programs. Many universities offer accelerated programs for veterans. Online programs from reputable schools (WGU, SNHU, ASU Online) are veteran-friendly.
veteran_discount_notes: GI Bill and Yellow Ribbon programs cover most master's programs

recommendation: If you were a combat medic (68W) or Corpsman (HM), your training likely exceeds civilian EMT standards. Check your state for military-to-EMT bridge programs.
veteran_discount_notes: NREMT has specific military pathway with reduced requirements

recommendation: Automation and configuration management tool. Free and open source. Your maintenance and system management experience translates.
Infrastructure as Code tool. Free training from HashiCorp. Your infrastructure management experience is relevant foundation.
Scrum is a specific Agile framework with sprints, standups, and retrospectives. If you ran daily briefs, weekly planning cycles, and AARs — that is essentially Scrum.
estimated_time: 2-4 hours to learn / 2 days for CSM

recommendation: If you wrote SOPs, maintenance procedures, technical manuals, naval messages, or operations orders, you have technical writing experience. Add it to your skills.
Next-gen firewall platform. Free Beacon training available. Your military firewall/network security experience is directly relevant.
Military COOP (Continuity of Operations) planning is business continuity. Your disaster preparedness and emergency planning experience translates directly.
Your military network experience maps to CCNA concepts. If you managed shipboard or tactical networks, you likely already know 70% of the material.
veteran_discount_notes: Cisco offers military/veteran training through NetAcad

recommendation: Penetration testing framework. Free Community Edition. If you have PenTest+ or military red team experience, Metasploit is the standard tool.

If you held a Secret clearance during military service, it may still be active or in the system. Check your JPAS/DISS record. An active clearance is extremely valuable — always list it on your resume.
Capability Maturity Model Integration. Used in defense contracting for process maturity. Your QA and process improvement experience is relevant.
Your PenTest+ covers similar offensive security concepts. Mention PenTest+ and any vulnerability assessment experience as equivalent.
veteran_discount_notes: EC-Council offers military discount through GI Bill and VET TEC

recommendation: With your military leadership experience, you likely already qualify for PMP. Military project management experience counts toward the 36-month requirement.
veteran_discount_notes: PMI offers $100 military discount. VET TEC covers training costs.

recommendation: Statistical programming language. Free courses available. Used heavily in data science and analytics roles.
OSCP is hands-on pentesting certification. Your PenTest+ and military cyber experience are relevant but OSCP requires dedicated lab time.
veteran_discount_notes: OffSec offers military/veteran pricing

recommendation: Your Security+ and management experience map to CISM domains. Your military leadership in security programs is directly relevant.
International Traffic in Arms Regulations. If you handled classified defense material or worked with defense systems, you operated under ITAR restrictions. Add to your resume.
TS/SCI access is the most valuable clearance level. If you had it, verify through your SSO or security manager. Even if expired, noting "TS/SCI (inactive, eligible for reinstatement)" has value.
Endpoint detection and response platform. Free CrowdStrike University training. Your security monitoring experience translates.
If you held TS during service, verify current status. TS clearances can remain active for up to 6 years. An active TS is worth $5-15K in additional salary. Always list on resume.
